label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out if following these instructions brings me back to the starting point. The options are Yes or No. Let me break it down step by step. First, the instructions say to always face forward. So my direction is fixed; I don't turn left or right, just move left or right while facing forward. Wait, actually, when you take steps right or left, does that mean changing direction? Hmm, maybe I need to clarify. If I'm facing forward, taking a step right would mean moving sideways to the right, keeping the same forward direction. So my orientation doesn't change, just my position. So I can model this as movements on a coordinate grid. Let me assign coordinates. Let's say the starting point is (0,0). Facing forward is, let's say, the positive y-direction. But when moving left or right, that would be along the x-axis. Wait, but if I take steps right, that's moving along the x-axis to the right (positive x), and left would be negative x. Moving forward or backward is along the y-axis. Forward is positive y, backward is negative y. So let's track each movement: 1. Start at (0,0), facing forward (let's say direction is along positive y-axis). 2. Take 3 steps right. Since facing forward is along y, moving right would be positive x direction. So each step right adds +1 to x. 3 steps right: x = 0 + 3 = 3, y remains 0. Position: (3, 0). 3. Take 1 step backward. Backward is negative y direction. So y = 0 - 1 = -1. Position: (3, -1). 4. Take 6 steps left. Left is negative x direction. So x = 3 - 6 = -3. Position: (-3, -1). 5. Take 1 step forward. Forward is positive y direction. So y = -1 + 1 = 0. Position: (-3, 0). 6. Take 3 steps right. Right is positive x direction. x = -3 + 3 = 0. Position: (0, 0). Wait, so after all these steps, the final position is (0,0), which is the starting point. So the answer should be Yes. But let me double-check each step to make sure I didn't make a mistake. Starting at (0,0): - 3 right: (3,0) - 1 back: (3,-1) -
